_comment: Per-bank additions to extract_statement.py's base header-token vocabulary and footer noise-skip list. Most new banks won't need an entry here at all — the base vocabulary already recognizes common wording (Description/Particulars/Narration, Debit/Withdrawal, Credit/Deposit, Balance, Reference/Cheque). Only add an entry if a bank uses column-header wording or footer text the base lists don't already cover. extra_header_tokens keys are lowercase-letters-only tokens (same normalization extract_statement.py already applies) mapping to one of: txn_date, value_date, description, reference, debit, credit, balance. extra_exclude_patterns are lowercase substrings to skip as footer/summary noise, same as the base EXCLUDE_PATTERNS list. column_anchor_overrides is a rare escape hatch: an explicit x-position (points from the page's left edge, as measured via pdfplumber) for a field, only needed when a bank centers its header labels within a wide column instead of left-aligning them with the actual data underneath (for text fields), or when the real right-aligned data's right edge sits further out than the header label's own right edge (for amount fields) — determined by testing against a real sample statement, not guessable from the header text alone. excluded_fields is for a column whose header label exists but which carries zero real data on this bank's layout (e.g. an always-blank Cheque/Reference No. column) — excluding it stops its position from stealing width from a neighboring column that does have long real content.
